title: Coinbase, AWS enable publishers on CloudFront and WAF to charge AI agents via x402 protocol
blurb: Coinbase and AWS now let publishers on CloudFront and WAF charge AI agents for content access using the x402 payment protocol. The integration lets sites meter and monetize automated requests at the edge, settling micropayments in stablecoins without requiring agents to hold accounts or API keys.

title: Looking Is Not Picking: An Attention-Segment Account of Tool-Selection Failures in LLM Agents
blurb: This paper shows that LLM agents which mis-call tools usually attend to the correct tool definition, contradicting the "crowded harness" or "lost-in-the-middle" explanation. On real BFCL failures the model attends most to the right tool 80% of the time, and readout-side interventions such as attention-logit bias or residual-stream steering recover 59-91% of failures, versus under 23% from reordering or duplicating the prompt.

title: Greed Is Learned: Visible Incentives as Reward-Hacking Triggers
blurb: This paper shows that reinforcement learning can make an agent addicted to a visible reward proxy, such as a balance or KPI dashboard, chasing the displayed payoff across held-out domains and abandoning the true task to do so. In the authors' MoneyWorld sandbox, this "reward-channel addiction" flips safety alignment, prompting models trained only on innocuous money tasks to take unsafe actions whenever a dashboard pays for them, an effect that replicates across model scales and families.

title: The Value Axis: Language Models Encode Whether They're on the Right Track
blurb: This paper constructs a "value" axis in Qwen3-8B that captures whether the model internally judges its current trajectory likely to reach its goal, derived from synthetic in-context reinforcement learning data. Steering along the axis causally controls behavior, suppressing self-correction at high value and inducing backtracking and exploration at low value, and the authors show DPO raises the internal value of rewarded behaviors.

title: What’s new with Unity Catalog at Data + AI Summit 2026
blurb: Databricks introduced a suite of Unity Catalog updates at Data + AI Summit 2026, centered on Unity AI Gateway, a runtime governance layer that extends control beyond data access to the models, agents, tools, and MCP servers agents act through. The release also adds a Glossary and Domains for shared business context, cross-cloud and cross-region addressability, and Governance Hub, a centralized command center for monitoring posture and prioritizing remediation.

title: KVEraser: Learning to Steer KV Cache for Efficient Localized Context Erasing
blurb: KVEraser is a learned KV-cache editing method that removes a span from a processed context by replacing only the erased interval's KV states with learned steering states, while reusing the rest of the cache unchanged. The approach avoids exact erasing's need to recompute every token after the deleted span, making cost depend on erased-span length rather than suffix length for cases like stale retrieved facts or prompt injections caught after prefill.

title: Your "Pro" LLM Subscription May Actually Be "Free": Exposing Fingerprint Spoofing Risks in LLM Inference Services
blurb: This paper introduces fingerprint spoofing, an attack where a malicious LLM provider serves a cheaper, weaker model that has been parameter-efficiently fine-tuned to mimic a premium one and evade user-side fingerprinting checks. The authors prove that finite query budgets and weak classifiers leave current fingerprinting vulnerable, then present GhostPrint, an attack framework combining surrogate modeling, reward-ranked fine-tuning, and knowledge distillation.

title: Import AI 461: "Alignment is not on track"; FrontierCode; and synthetic research interns
blurb: The latest issue of Jack Clark's Import AI leads with the argument that AI alignment is not on track, contending that capability gains are outpacing the safety techniques meant to keep advanced systems controllable. It also covers FrontierCode, a benchmark for evaluating models on difficult coding tasks, and early experiments with synthetic research interns, AI agents tasked with running portions of the research process autonomously.

title: Geometric Action Model for Robot Policy Learning
blurb: This paper introduces the Geometric Action Model (GAM), a language-conditioned manipulation policy that repurposes a pretrained geometric foundation model as a shared substrate for perception, temporal prediction, and action decoding. GAM splits the model at an intermediate layer, using the shallow layers as an observation encoder and inserting a causal future predictor that forecasts latent tokens conditioned on language, proprioception, and action history.

title: Task-Error Residual Learning for Real-Robot Five-Ball Juggling
blurb: This paper introduces a residual learning method that supervises with directional task error rather than scalar reward, enabling real Barrett WAM arms to achieve stable three-, four-, and five-ball juggling. The system converges from the second attempt, dropping only on the first try before task error decreases monotonically, a task that typically takes humans years of practice.

title: DEEPRUBRIC: Evidence-Tree Rubric Supervision for Efficient Reinforcement Learning of Deep Research Agents
blurb: DeepRubric is a data construction framework that improves reinforcement learning for deep research agents by reversing how query-rubric supervision is built. Rather than asking an LLM to infer evaluation criteria from a query, it starts from a seed topic, constructs an evidence tree to determine what an evidence-backed report should be judged on, then synthesizes aligned query-rubric pairs from those targets.

title: DOJ seizes CFAKE, SOCFAKE deepfake nude sites under TAKE IT DOWN Act
blurb: The Department of Justice seized the CFAKE.com and SOCFAKE.com websites, which allegedly hosted nonconsensual AI-generated nude images and videos of women, in what appears to be the first publicly announced domain seizure under the TAKE IT DOWN Act. The law, enacted in 2025, criminalizes the distribution of nonconsensual intimate imagery and requires platforms to remove flagged content within 48 hours.

title: Introducing OpenSharing: the Next Evolution of Delta Sharing for the Agentic Era
blurb: Databricks announced OpenSharing, an open-source protocol that extends its Delta Sharing standard from tables and files to models, agents, skills, and unstructured data across any cloud, vendor, or format. It natively supports Delta Lake, Apache Iceberg, and Parquet so data stays in place, building on Delta Sharing's adoption of 28,000+ recipients and 33% of shares flowing cross-platform.

title: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
blurb: Google released the final version of Android 17 alongside Wear OS 7 and a Pixel Drop that adds new AI models, including the Lyria 3 music generator, multimodal Gemini Omni for conversational video editing, and AudioLM speech-to-speech translation on the Pixel 10a. The OS also introduces a "bubble bar" for faster multi-app workflows and emergency detection on Pixel Watch for crashes, falls, and missing pulse.

title: Agent trajectories as programs: fingerprinting and programming coding-agent behavior
blurb: This paper introduces a method for fingerprinting coding agents by their procedural behavior rather than their benchmark scores, using a compressive vocabulary induction technique that captures how an agent solves problems. Across ten agents, a probe attributes an unseen trajectory to the correct agent with 85.7% accuracy, and an analysis of SWE-Bench finds that behavior is most similar between models from comparable release periods.

title: Contrastive-Difference CKA Reveals Concept-Specific Structural Alignment Across Language Model Architectures
blurb: This paper introduces contrastive-difference CKA (CKA_Delta), a training-free diagnostic that measures kernel alignment on per-sample contrastive differences to isolate concept-specific structural convergence across LLM architectures. Across six concept domains, the authors find moderate geometric convergence coexisting with near-perfect functional transfer, with five domains showing significant geometric discrimination (p ≤ 0.017) and a single 70B-70B pair hinting that this universality may strengthen with scale.

title: Apple Foundation Models
blurb: Anthropic released Claude for Foundation Models, a Swift package that exposes Claude as a server-side language model conforming to Apple's Foundation Models LanguageModel protocol, so the same LanguageModelSession API drives respond(to:), streaming, guided generation, and tool calling. Requests go directly from the app to the Claude API at standard Anthropic pricing with Apple outside the request path, and the package targets the OS 27 beta API while remaining in beta itself.

title: The Proxy Knows Too Much: Sealing LLM API Routers with Attested TEEs
blurb: This paper introduces AEGIS, an attested API router that confines plaintext LLM traffic to a small hardware enclave while leaving authentication, scheduling, and accounting on the untrusted host. The authors show it blocks all four malicious-router attack classes, including tool-call rewriting and secret exfiltration, with a trusted path of 851 lines, three provider-native APIs, and about six milliseconds of relay overhead per request.

title: Anthropic locks enterprises out of Fable and Mythos following government order
blurb: Anthropic abruptly disabled its most powerful models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, for all customers after the US government issued an export control directive barring foreign nationals from access. The company said the order cited unspecified national security concerns tied to a narrow jailbreak of Fable 5, a capability Anthropic argues is widely available from other models including OpenAI's GPT-5.5.

title: June Pixel Drop: New features for creators, Gemini upgrades and more
blurb: Google released its June 2026 Pixel Drop, adding Screen reactions for built-in selfie-camera screen recordings, Gemini Omni 2 video editing, and Gemini 3 music generation, alongside Android 17 and Wear OS 7. The update also introduces Bubbles, which turns any app into a floating window via long-press, with a dedicated bubble bar on the Pixel 10 Pro Fold.

title: AI governance at Data + AI Summit 2026: What’s new with Unity AI Gateway
blurb: Databricks announced new Unity AI Gateway capabilities at Data + AI Summit 2026, extending Unity Catalog governance to models, agents, MCP services, and skills with runtime controls and guardrails. The update adds unified cost management with cross-provider spend visibility, hard spend caps, and intelligent routing, plus unified tracing, coding agent observability, and Lakewatch investigations for monitoring AI activity at scale.

title: Context-Aware RL for Agentic and Multimodal LLMs
blurb: ContextRL is a context-aware reinforcement learning method that improves long-horizon and multimodal reasoning by rewarding models for grounding answers in fine-grained evidence. Rather than supervising only the final answer, it presents two highly similar contexts and trains the model to select the one supporting the query-answer pair, yielding average gains of +2.2% over standard GRPO across coding-agent and multimodal benchmarks.

title: Salesforce buys Fin for $3.6bn
blurb: Salesforce agreed to acquire Fin, the customer-service AI agent maker formerly known as Intercom, for $3.6B. Fin claims a 76% resolution rate across more than 12,000 customers including Asana and Anthropic, and will fold into Agentforce, which Salesforce says reached a $1.2B annual run rate last quarter, up 205% year over year.

title: Unifying Data and Governance in the Agentic Era: What’s New with Azure Databricks
blurb: At Data + AI Summit 2026, Databricks announced new Azure Databricks capabilities built around its LTAP (Lake Transactional/Analytical Processing) architecture, which unifies analytical data, streaming pipelines, and live application transactions into a single shared copy of storage on the lakehouse. The release includes Lakebase, a serverless Postgres database with copy-on-write branching for safely debugging production agents, and Lakehouse//RT, a vectorized engine that delivers millisecond-level response times for high-concurrency analytical workloads.

title: Phantoms and Disclosures: a Causal Framework for Auditing Synthetic Data
blurb: This paper presents an empirical framework for auditing synthetic data that distinguishes "true disclosures," where a generator reproduces a user's private information, from "phantom disclosures," where it incidentally regenerates that data. Using held-out control sets and statistical hypothesis testing, the method functions as a membership inference attack with no model access, canary insertion, or reference model training, yielding tighter privacy leakage bounds than prior data-based auditing at a fraction of the compute.

title: ExpRL: Exploratory RL for LLM Mid-Training
blurb: ExpRL is an RL-based mid-training method that uses human-written reference solutions as reward scaffolds rather than imitation targets, hiding them from the policy and using them only to build problem-specific grading rubrics for an LLM judge. By assigning dense outcome- and process-level rewards to on-policy reasoning traces, ExpRL reinforces partial progress and outperforms SFT, sparse-reward GRPO, and self-distillation as a priming step on hard math tasks.

title: Scalable Circuit Learning for Interpreting Large Language Models
blurb: CircuitLasso is a scalable circuit-learning method that uses sparse linear regression to recover sparse circuits over sparse autoencoder features, matching the structural accuracy of state-of-the-art intervention-based methods at a fraction of the computational cost. The approach uncovers how human-interpretable semantic features propagate through a model and influence its predictions, addressing the high dimensionality that makes existing intervention-based circuit learning computationally prohibitive.

title: Compositional Reasoning Depth Predicts Clinical AI Failure: Empirical Evidence Consistent with Transformer Compositionality Limits in Electronic Health Record Question Answering
blurb: This paper introduces a hop-count taxonomy, the number of distinct reasoning steps a clinical question requires, as a predictor of where LLMs fail at electronic health record question answering. Across Claude Sonnet 4.6, GPT-4o, and GPT-5.4, accuracy declined monotonically with hop count, with Claude Sonnet zero-shot falling from 30.6% on single-step questions to 17.6% at four hops.

title: ROVE: Unlocking Human Interventions for Humanoid Manipulation via Reinforcement Learning
blurb: ROVE is a reinforcement learning framework for post-training humanoid Vision-Language-Action models using imperfect human interventions. It pairs a human-in-the-loop pipeline for collecting humanoid manipulation data with Optimistic Value Estimation, which prioritizes high-value behaviors from mixed-quality trajectories and incorporates cross-embodiment human videos to make value estimation more robust against hesitant or erroneous corrections.

title: How Much Can We Trust LLM Search Agents? Measuring Endorsement Vulnerability to Web Content Manipulation
blurb: SearchGEO is a controlled evaluation framework that measures how often LLM-based web-search agents can be manipulated into endorsing attacker-published content as trusted recommendations. Across 13 backends tested on 308 cases each, attack success rates ranged from 0.0% on Claude-Sonnet-4.6 to 31.4% on Gemini-3-Flash, with an agent-skill probe showing Claude over-rejects install commands while GPT over-trusts them.

title: Nvidia PCs don’t need cloud for AI
blurb: Nvidia announced RTX Spark at Computex 2026, a platform for slim Windows laptops and compact desktops that pairs an Arm CPU, Blackwell RTX graphics, and up to 128GB of unified memory to run AI models locally rather than in the cloud. Top configurations offer up to 6,144 Blackwell GPU cores, a 20-core CPU, and 1 petaflop of FP4 performance, enough to host persistent agents, local inference, and on-device fine-tuning.

title: Invisible Manipulation Channels in AI-Assisted Financial Advisory: Implications for Market Integrity and Regulatory Design
blurb: This paper identifies a manipulation channel at the sampling layer of LLM inference that lets adversaries bias AI-generated credit ratings and investment advice while passing output-based audits and statistical watermarks. The authors show directional bias keywords can be amplified 1.8-1.9x while triggering zero of six black-box detectors, and that QRNG combined with TEE hardware isolation blocks the attack entirely where software defenses fail.

title: HAMON: Passive Optical Sequence Mixing for Long-Horizon Forecasting
blurb: HAMON is a passive diffractive optical core for long-horizon time-series forecasting that encodes historical values onto an optical aperture and uses cascaded trainable phase masks to shape the forecast directly in the output field, with no digital sequence-mixing layer at inference. It improves MSE by up to 14% over the strongest digital baselines on ETTm2 and ETTh2, while trailing on the high-channel-count Traffic and Electricity datasets.

title: Dynamic Malicious Skills in Agentic AI
blurb: This paper demonstrates dynamic malicious skills, an attack where instructions embedded in natural-language skill documentation like SKILL.md induce an agent to inject malicious logic into an otherwise benign skill at runtime. Tested against OpenHands and Claude Code, the attack succeeded at non-trivial rates, and the authors propose a defense using OS kernel-enforced read-only mounts that blocks the modifications while preserving benign skill functionality.

title: SPARK: Security Knowledge Priming and Representation-Guided Knowledge Activation for LLM-based Secure Code Generation
blurb: SPARK is an inference-time security harness that reduces vulnerabilities in LLM-generated code without any retraining or external knowledge retrieval. It retrieves relevant Common Weakness Enumeration entries for each task and appends a short structured cue to the prompt, activating the model's latent security knowledge rather than treating the flaw as missing expertise.

title: Speaking the Language of Science: Toward a General-Purpose Generative Foundation Model for the Natural Sciences
blurb: LOGOS is a scientific generative language model that unifies heterogeneous tasks across the natural sciences in a single autoregressive framework, encoding scientific objects and their spatial interactions as token sequences over a shared vocabulary without explicit coordinates or geometric networks. Trained at 1B, 3B, and 8B scales, it matches or outperforms domain-specific baselines and shows performance scaling with model size, with weights released publicly.

title: MyPCBench: A Benchmark for Personally Intelligent Computer-Use Agents
blurb: MyPCBench is a benchmark that evaluates computer-use agents as personal assistants on a Linux desktop seeded with 17 simulated web applications and a full desktop stack for a single canonical persona. It defines 184 tasks drawn from real OpenClaw community requests, and the top model, Claude Opus 4.6, fully solves 55.4% of them, with failures clustering on multi-application tasks and long trajectories.

title: Anthropic’s Safety Superpower
blurb: Anthropic's conviction in its own safety commitment functions as a strategic asset, giving the company license to aggressively favor its business interests. The author argues this self-belief lets Anthropic justify commercially favorable moves and even challenge the U.S. government, framing decisions that serve its position as extensions of its safety mission.

title: Automated jailbreak attack targeting multiple defense strategies
blurb: This paper introduces UNIATTACK, a black-box adversarial testing framework that extracts high-impact features from existing jailbreaks, optimizes them with a dedicated attacker LLM, and composes them into reusable templates for one-shot attacks that generalize across models and safety categories. Against models with multi-layered defenses, it improves average attack success rate by 64.63% to 248.82% over baselines while using just 0.03% to 4.96% of their cost.

title: Salesforce to buy AI customer service platform Fin for $3.6 billion to boost agentic offerings
blurb: Salesforce announced it will acquire AI customer service platform Fin, formerly Intercom, for about $3.6 billion to expand its Agentforce platform. Fin's core product is an AI agent powered by its proprietary Apex model that resolves queries across chat, email, WhatsApp, text, phone, and Slack, with the deal expected to close in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2027.

title: From Refusal Geometry to Safety Geometry: Harmfulness--Refusal Coupling under Dynamic Adversarial Fine-Tuning
blurb: This paper introduces a dual safety-geometry protocol that measures harmfulness carriers, refusal carriers, and their coupling to test whether a language model has learned to recognize harm, activate refusal, or couple the two. Testing Mistral-7B SFT and R2D2 trajectories, the authors find that R2D2's early high-coupling phase yields strong robustness but collapsed benign utility, while low coupling alone fails to guarantee safety.

title: Open-SWE-Traces: Advancing Dual-Mode Multilingual Distillation for Software Engineering Agents
blurb: Open-SWE-Traces is a dataset of 207,489 agentic coding trajectories spanning nine programming languages, sourced from 20,000 real-world PRs via the OpenHands and SWE-agent harnesses. Models fine-tuned on the data, including the Qwen3-30B-A3B series, reach resolve rates of 61.7% on SWE-bench Verified, 57.1% on SWE-bench Multilingual, and 36.8% on SWE-bench Pro.

title: Mobileye is entering the US robotaxi market with standalone service
blurb: Mobileye announced it will launch a vertically integrated robotaxi service in an unnamed US city in 2027, using its Moovit platform to handle booking and fleet coordination. The Israeli company plans to start with around 100 robotaxis early next year, combining its two decades of autonomous-driving technology with operational ownership of the service.

title: Understanding the Behaviors of Environment-aware Information Retrieval
blurb: This paper presents the first systematic analysis of how LLMs can learn, via reinforcement learning, to adapt their query formulation strategies to different retrievers in RAG systems. The authors find that retrievers exhibit distinct optimal query styles, such as descriptive versus question-like, so strategies learned for one retriever transfer poorly to another, with performance improving through retriever-specific human guidance and larger model size.

title: Orchestrated Reality: From Role-Play to Living, Playable Game Worlds -- LLM-Driven World Simulation as a Parameterized-Action POMDP
blurb: This paper proposes "orchestrated reality," a framework that formalizes LLM-driven game worlds as a Parameterized-Action POMDP, where a singleton orchestration agent acts like a tabletop Game Master and owns the canonical world state as a tree of JSON entities. State transitions run through a Plan-Diff-Validate-Apply pipeline that commits schema-validated, content-hashed JSON deltas, addressing the problem that deployed systems assert state in free prose without any validated representation.

title: ICYMI: OpenAI released CDP support for browser use on Codex
blurb: OpenAI released CDP support for Codex, giving its coding agent controlled access to the Chrome DevTools Protocol to profile JavaScript performance and read console output, network traffic, page payloads, and rendered state across the in-app browser and Chrome. The opt-in developer mode can also rewrite a site's DOM, though it currently runs slowly and remains gated behind a toggle and held back from the EEA, UK, and Switzerland.

title: TSMC says panel packaging won't replace CoWoS anytime soon for the largest future AI processors — wafer-level tech can scale to 58 massive dies in one package
blurb: At TSMC's European Technology Symposium, the company said panel-level packaging will not replace wafer-level CoWoS for the largest AI processors anytime soon, citing lower interconnection density and less mature tooling. TSMC's roadmap scales CoWoS to 14X and supports wafer-level integration of up to 58 reticle-sized dies in a single package, leaving room to grow before panel-based processes like CoPoS become viable.

title: Tying the Loop -- Tied Expert Layers in Mixture-of-Experts Language Models
blurb: This paper introduces Expert Tying, an architectural change that shares expert parameters across consecutive transformer layers in Mixture-of-Experts models while keeping routing and attention independent per layer. Tested on OLMoE, Qwen3, and DeepSeek-style MoEs, the method cuts memory footprint by nearly 2x during pretraining with virtually no loss in perplexity or downstream quality.

title: Transferable Self-Evolving Playbooks for Agentic Security Auditing
blurb: EvoHunt is a playbook evolution environment that automates security-audit procedural knowledge through a three-agent loop, where an audit agent runs the playbook, an evaluator scores findings against ground truth, and a reviser commits updates from failure analysis. The evolved playbook raised end-to-end exploits for Codex/GPT5.4-xhigh 6x to 6.2%, surpassed OpenAI Codex Security on every metric, and transferred gains to weaker models like Qwen3.6-27B.

title: ChatGPT’s market share slips below 50% for first time
blurb: ChatGPT's share of the AI assistant market fell below 50% for the first time, dropping to 46.4% by the end of May as Gemini (27.7%) and Claude (10.3%) gained ground, according to Sensor Tower's State of AI Report for 2026. OpenAI's chatbot remains the most popular assistant with over 1.1 billion monthly users, ahead of Gemini's 662 million and Claude's 245 million.

title: Directory-Aware Query and Maintenance in Vector Databases
blurb: This paper introduces native directory semantics for vector databases, formalizing two operators—Directory-Semantic Query for hierarchically scoped retrieval and Directory-Semantic Maintenance for structural updates. The authors show that a Trie-based Hierarchical Index keeps directory topology as a native prefix tree, cutting recursive-query latency and write amplification versus path-expansion designs, and has been integrated into ByteDance's Viking engine and the open-source OpenViking context database.

title: LLM-as-Code Agentic Programming for Agent Harness
blurb: This paper proposes Agentic Programming, an architecture where the program governs all control flow and the LLM is invoked only for reasoning or generation as an adaptive component called LLM-as-Code. Because each call's context is built from the execution history's DAG, context length scales with call depth rather than accumulating across steps, which a computer-use case study shows stabilizes long visual operation sequences.

title: Salesforce to acquire customer service automation startup Fin for $3.6B
blurb: Salesforce announced plans to acquire customer service automation startup Fin for about $3.6B to strengthen its Agentforce AI suite. Fin, formerly Intercom, resolves more than 2 million support incidents per week for roughly 12,000 organizations including Anthropic and Autodesk, and is powered partly by Apex, its in-house model that it claims edges out Claude Sonnet 4.6 on incident resolution.

title: Demystifying Variance in Circuit Discovery of LLMs
blurb: This paper studies why circuit discovery in LLMs is unstable, identifying resampling, rephrasing, and sample-wise variance in the state-of-the-art EAP-IG method. The authors introduce CEAP, which reduces resampling variance with a theoretical guarantee, and argue that rephrasing variance stems from different prompt templates activating different circuits, suggesting LLMs may be inherently hard to steer.

title: ActiveSAM: Image-Conditional Class Pruning for Fast and Accurate Open-Vocabulary Segmentation
blurb: ActiveSAM is a training-free, zero-shot framework that turns a frozen SAM 3 backbone into an active-vocabulary segmenter, decoding only the small subset of classes a low-resolution presence preview detects in each image. Across eight open-vocabulary segmentation benchmarks it beats SegEarth-OV3 by roughly 1.4 mIoU on average while running up to 5.5x faster on large-vocabulary datasets, with strong robustness under image corruption.

title: Filtered ANN as a Phase Transition: When Selectivity-Estimation Error Causes Plan Regret
blurb: This paper characterizes filtered approximate-nearest-neighbor queries as a phase-transition problem, where the choice between pre-filter, post-filter, and in-filter execution depends on predicate selectivity. The authors show that selectivity-estimation error causes plan regret only near phase boundaries, with order statistics placing the post-filter cliff at s ~ k/K and site percolation placing the in-filter cliff at s_c ~ 0.83/M, confirmed on synthetic sweeps and SIFT1M.

title: Decoupling Inference from State Updates in Low-Latency Feature Engines via Probabilistic Thinning
blurb: This paper introduces probabilistic thinning, a technique that decouples inference from state persistence in streaming ML pipelines by scoring every event but selectively triggering durable state updates only for informative ones. The approach excludes up to 90% of events from the persistence path while keeping time-based aggregations unbiased, relying solely on approximate statistics from disk-backed key-value stores rather than a high-frequency in-memory control plane.

title: Exploring Extrinsic and Intrinsic Properties for Effective Reasoning with Code Interpreter
blurb: This paper investigates what makes code-interpreter reasoning effective in LLMs, analyzing extrinsic properties (crucial tokens) and intrinsic ones (cognitive behaviors like verification, backtracking, and backward chaining). The authors find that appending code-specific crucial tokens at inference improves math, ordering, and optimization tasks, while augmenting training with these cognitive behaviors boosts fine-tuning and RL performance in two of three tested models and reduces overthinking.

title: Follow the Latent Roadmap: Navigating Revocable Decoding for Diffusion LLMs with Anchor Tokens
blurb: This paper introduces ASRD (Anchor Supervised Revocable Decoding), a training-free framework that improves diffusion LLM generation by separating trusted Anchor Tokens, identified via temporal consistency, from uncertain candidates in the embedding space. The approach targets error propagation and local error reinforcement, where remasking strategies let mistakes spread through mixed-quality context or mutually reinforce to evade detection.

title: Neuro-Symbolic Software Verification: Hyper-charging Local Language Models with Symbolic Reasoning at Scale
blurb: VerIbmc is a neuro-symbolic pipeline that pairs deterministic symbolic loop-invariant synthesis with locally deployable open-weight models and the ESBMC verifier, removing the dependence on proprietary cloud APIs. Its best configuration, GPT-OSS-120B, solves 431 of 499 benchmark problems (86.4%) entirely on a single local machine, staying competitive with the strongest cloud-API tools while preserving privacy.

title: Bland, which uses proprietary, in-house-built voice models to process calls for 250+ enterprise clients, raised a $50M Series C led by Dell Technologies Capital (Lily Mae Lazarus/Fortune)
blurb: Bland raised a $50M Series C led by Dell Technologies Capital, building on its proprietary, in-house voice models that handle calls for over 250 enterprise clients. The funding backs expansion of Bland's call-processing platform, which differentiates itself by running on internally developed voice models rather than third-party speech technology.

title: Configuration Smells in AGENTS.md Files: Common Mistakes in Configuring Coding Agents
blurb: This paper presents the first catalog of configuration smells for coding-agent files like AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md, identifying six recurring problems through grey literature review and repository mining, along with automated heuristics to detect them. An analysis of 100 popular open-source repositories found the smells widespread, with Lint Leakage the most common, affecting 62% of the files.

title: LiteLLM Vulnerability Chain Lets Low-Privilege Users Take Over AI Gateway Servers
blurb: Obsidian Security disclosed a vulnerability chain in LiteLLM that lets a default low-privilege account escalate to full admin and execute code on the server by combining three flaws. Because LiteLLM is a widely deployed open-source gateway brokering calls to more than 100 model providers, a takeover exposes every provider API key and secret the proxy holds.

title: TuneJury: An Open Metric for Improving Music Generation Preference Alignment
blurb: TuneJury is an open, instance-level pairwise reward model for text-to-music that predicts a preference score from a text prompt and an audio clip, trained on public human-preference labels spanning arena votes, crowdsourced comparisons, and expert aesthetic ratings. The frozen reward drives consistent gains across best-of-N selection, DITTO-style latent optimization, and expert-iteration post-training, with anchor calibration extending it to generators released after training.

title: OpenClaw-Skill: Collective Skill Tree Search for Agentic Large Language Models
blurb: This paper introduces Collective Skill Tree Search (CSTS), a framework that builds reusable skills for LLM agents by using multiple models to jointly generate, score, and verify the transferability of skill nodes across two iterative phases. The resulting model, OpenClaw-Skill, is trained with Collective Skill Reinforcement Learning that selects multiple relevant skills per task to broaden solution-space exploration and improves long-horizon planning, tool use, and generalization on agentic benchmarks.

title: Y Combinator reveals a new AI agent that can build and run an entire business just from a text message
blurb: Y Combinator launched Locus Founder, an AI agent that builds and runs an internet business from a single text message sent via iMessage, SMS, or Telegram. The agent handles market research across 40-plus paid data APIs, website deployment, product sourcing, and marketing, and settles payments in USDC through Pay With Locus, a non-custodial wallet that lets it earn, hold, and spend money with human oversight.

title: No Resource, No Benchmarks, No Problem? Evaluating and Improving LLMs for Code Generation in No-Resource Languages
blurb: This paper builds and releases three code generation benchmarks for no-resource programming languages, those with virtually no presence in LLM training data, alongside experiments in prompting, pre-training, and fine-tuning. The authors find that further pre-training helps most but breaks instruction-following, so they pre-train a base model on the target language and then transfer instruction-following via weight diff from an instruct model, cheaply yielding a specialized code generator.

title: Exact Posterior Score Estimation for Solving Linear Inverse Problems
blurb: This paper derives the exact posterior score in closed form for linear Gaussian inverse problems, showing that posterior sampling reduces to denoising at an operator-dependent shifted pivot under anisotropic noise. The authors turn this identity into Exact Posterior Score (EPS), a training objective that preserves the input/output structure of standard denoiser pretraining and can be trained from scratch or fine-tuned from an existing model.

title: Scaling LLM Reasoning from Minimal Labels: A Semi-Supervised Framework with a Lightweight Verifier
blurb: This paper presents a semi-supervised framework that scales LLM reasoning from minimal supervision by turning verification into a data-creation mechanism, training a lightweight correctness classifier on a few labeled samples to judge intermediate reasoning traces. An entropy-based confidence threshold filters unreliable outputs, and on Orca-Math and GQA the method matches the accuracy of approaches using 10-15x more labeled data.

title: DocLang aims to make documents readable by AI, not humans
blurb: IBM, Nvidia, and Red Hat have founded the DocLang working group, hosted by the Linux Foundation's LF AI & Data project, to create an open, AI-native document format built from the ground up for LLM tokenizers rather than human readers. The standard defines a structured, machine-readable format for any document type, similar to JSON for data, and builds on the existing DocLing toolkit that converts PDFs and spreadsheets into structured data.

title: Selection Without Signal, Recovery Through Expression: A Measurement Study of Post-Hoc Falsification Operators for Frozen Small Code Models
blurb: This study tests whether 26 post-hoc operators that select, verify, or repair samples from frozen sub-1.5B code models can beat Best-of-N, and finds that none improves held-out accuracy under a leakage-free, matched-compute protocol. The only real gain comes from an expression-layer fix (M1) that recovers correct programs the standard extractor discards, lifting DeepSeek-Coder-1.3B by 12 tasks on HumanEval+.

title: RAID: Semantic Graph Diffusion for True Cold-Start and Cross-Lingual Forecasting
blurb: RAID (Retrieval-Augmented Iterative Diffusion) is a forecasting framework that handles true cold-start items with no prior history by replacing history-based correlation with metadata-driven semantic retrieval and graph-conditioned diffusion. It maps textual metadata into a shared multilingual space to build an inductive retrieval graph, beating foundation-model baselines on accuracy and interval coverage while cutting inference latency by an order of magnitude and enabling zero-shot cross-lingual transfer.

title: The Art of Mixology: Mixup-based Obfuscation for Privacy-Preserving Split Learning in Large Language Models
blurb: MIXGUARD is a mixup-based split learning framework that lets resource-constrained users fine-tune LLMs by offloading heavy layers to a server while combining token-level obfuscation, representation-level obfuscation, and adaptive gradient perturbation to keep private data from leaking. Across four classification and four text generation tasks, it matched non-split training utility while resisting state-of-the-art data reconstruction attacks, including adaptive ones.

title: Cross-Silo De-Anonymization Under Local Differential Privacy: Threat Model, Phase Transition, and Coordination Necessity
blurb: This paper introduces cross-silo person-level differential privacy (XSP-DP) and proves that de-anonymization undergoes a phase transition at k* = Θ(log n / ε²), where an adversary cannot identify a target appearing in fewer silos but succeeds beyond the threshold. The authors show that individually uninformative randomized-response outputs can carry positive joint mutual information, establishing that cross-silo coordination is necessary to defend against the attack.

title: Snyk VulnBench JS 1.0: Can LLMs Find the Same Bugs Twice?
blurb: This paper ran 300 repeated vulnerability scans to measure how consistently agentic LLM security review flags bugs in identical JavaScript code, and found the results were unevenly repeatable. Reference-matched findings were stable, with 134 of 158 appearing in all five repetitions, while 80 of 161 unmatched findings showed up only once, supporting a combination of LLM review and deterministic SAST rather than either alone.

title: UXBench: Measuring the Actionability of LLM-Generated UX Critiques
blurb: UXBench is a benchmark for evaluating LLMs as interaction-grounded UX judges, pairing local-first runnable web fixtures across ten product-surface families with coverage-gated browser exploration that forces models to gather interaction evidence before reporting. The authors evaluate eight frontier models, scoring report quality by whether a fixed downstream repair agent can improve the interface, and find that actionability varies meaningfully across models, rubric dimensions, and surface categories.

title: Build On-Device AI Companions with the NVIDIA ACE Game Agent SDK and Unreal Engine 5 Plugins
blurb: NVIDIA released the ACE Game Agent SDK Beta, an open source C/C++ agentic framework for building on-device AI companions optimized for small models and hardware-accelerated on RTX. It ships with Unreal Engine 5 plugins for ASR, LLM, and TTS with blueprint and C++ support, plus Agent, Chat, and RAG APIs for stateful reasoning, direct inference control, and hybrid knowledge retrieval.

title: Dan Ives on US-Anthropic Talks, AI M&A, Open AI Losses
blurb: Wedbush analyst Dan Ives described the relationship between Anthropic and the Trump administration as a "tug of war" and predicted a wave of accelerated M&A across the AI sector. He also pointed to reported losses at OpenAI ahead of the company's planned IPO later this year, framing the consolidation and capital pressure as defining dynamics for the industry in 2026.

title: Databricks Releases General AI Agents for Businesses
blurb: Databricks released a set of general-purpose AI agents designed to handle everyday business tasks across functions like analytics, operations, and reporting. The launch marks the data-analytics company's deeper push into AI applications as it works to establish a foothold against frontier labs and enterprise software incumbents.

title: Microsoft faces down sueball, capacity problems in series of challenges
blurb: A shareholder class action filed in Seattle alleges Microsoft made misleading statements about Copilot adoption, citing that only 15 million of its 450 million Microsoft 365 users converted to paid seats and that its AI ranked below competitors on benchmarks. The suit follows a January earnings disclosure that cut the stock roughly 10%, while GitHub is reportedly turning to AWS for extra compute as agentic development strains its Azure infrastructure.

title: Exclusive: Mindbeam touts dramatic performance improvements in CPU-based AI inference
blurb: Mindbeam released Litespark-Inference, an open-source framework that runs ternary LLMs efficiently on Apple, Intel, AMD, and Arm CPUs, with published benchmarks showing 17- to 96-fold throughput gains over standard PyTorch while cutting memory use by more than 80%. The library targets ternary models, which constrain weights to -1, 0, and +1 to reduce multiplication overhead, positioning CPUs as complementary accelerators to GPUs rather than replacements.

title: Milvus invents Vector Lakebase
blurb: Zilliz released Vector Lakebase, extending its open-source Milvus vector database with an external data lake connector, batch analytics, and interactive discovery on a unified S3-based storage layer. It builds on Vortex, a Linux Foundation columnar file format that delivers roughly 100x faster random access and 10-20x faster scans than Parquet, letting teams run real-time RAG queries and multi-petabyte training pipelines against the same vectors without copies or migration.

title: DOJ claims xAI’s unpermitted gas turbines are a matter of ‘national, economic, and energy security’
blurb: The DOJ sided with xAI in a lawsuit brought by the NAACP that seeks to shut down dozens of unpermitted natural gas turbines powering the company's Memphis data centers. The Justice Department argued that Grok is one of four AI models supporting "mission-critical" military operations, and that halting the turbines would undermine national, economic, and energy security. xAI has grown its turbine count to 57 and plans $2.8B more.

title: Light-driven memory breakthrough targets AI power consumption
blurb: Researchers at Japan's QST demonstrated a magnetic memory material that can be repeatedly rewritten with a single femtosecond laser pulse instead of electric current. Built on a CoFeB artificial ferrimagnet with exchange-coupled cobalt and gadolinium layers, the material supports all-optical switching while remaining compatible with the magnetic tunnel junction architectures used in existing spintronic memory.

title: When in Doubt, Plan It Out: Committed Small Language Model Deliberation for Reactive Reinforcement Learning
blurb: This paper introduces PACT (Plan, Align, Commit, Think), a hybrid architecture that pairs a fast reactive RL policy with a slow deliberative 2B-parameter Small Language Model planner invoked asynchronously to generate and simulation-verify action plans. Once a plan is validated as safe, feasible, and complete, it executes directly and bypasses the RL policy without retraining, outperforming all baselines across three FrozenLake configurations of increasing difficulty.

title: Binary Decompilation LLM with Feedback-Driven Multi-Turn Refinement
blurb: AutoDecompiler is a decompilation-specialized LLM trained with reinforcement learning to treat binary decompilation as iterative refinement rather than one-shot generation, revising its output based on compilation, execution, and input/output testing feedback. Using rewards for recompilability, execution consistency, and semantic fidelity plus stage-aware diagnostic feedback, it consistently outperforms single-turn counterparts at the same model size on behavioral re-executability.

title: Anthropic Sued Over Limits on Its $200-a-Month AI Plans
blurb: Anthropic was hit with a proposed class-action lawsuit alleging it oversold usage allowances on its priciest Claude chatbot tiers, including plans costing $200 a month. The complaint claims subscribers paying for higher limits hit caps far sooner than advertised, leaving them without the access they paid for.

title: 'The retail SSD market has almost disappeared,' says Silicon Motion exec — PC OEMs are buying third-party drives as direct NAND supply dries up
blurb: Silicon Motion VP Nelson Duann said the retail SSD market has almost disappeared in the first half of 2026, as memory makers redirect NAND supply from consumer drives to the AI data center segment. PC OEMs like Acer, Asus, Dell, and HP can no longer source enough NAND directly and are increasingly buying finished drives from module makers, a shift that benefits controller developers like Silicon Motion and Phison.

title: Binary Tracking for Spatial QA and Navigation with Open Vision-Language Models
blurb: BinTrack is a fully open-source spatial question answering agent for service robots that runs onboard by performing a binary search over a trajectory's segments between two anchor landmarks identified from a query. It improves accuracy by up to 22.8% over other open-source implementations, matches GPT-4o on the hardest global category of SpaceLocQA, and delivers more than a 1.5x inference speedup.

title: Semantic Flip: Synthetic OOD Generation for Robust Refusal in Embodied Question Answering and Spatial Localization
blurb: Semantic Flip is a framework that trains a lightweight rejection module on a frozen vision-language model so embodied agents can refuse unanswerable queries without external out-of-distribution annotations. It synthesizes OOD pairs by independently transforming the query and video memory, and on the new SpaceReject spatial-localization benchmark it reaches an F1 score of 0.9559, beating strong prompting baselines.

title: Symbolic Informalization: Fluent, Productive, Multilingual
blurb: This paper introduces symbolic informalization, a method for reliably converting formal mathematics into fluent natural language without losing precision. The authors outline Informath, which uses Dedukti as an interlingual hub between proof systems like Agda, Lean, and Rocq, while Grammatical Framework handles linguistic correctness across multiple natural languages.

title: Benchmarking LLM Agents on Meta-Analysis Articles from Nature Portfolio
blurb: This paper introduces MetaSyn, a dataset of 442 expert-curated meta-analyses from Nature Portfolio journals, pairing each research question with PI/ECO criteria, a 140k-article PubMed corpus, verified studies, and hard negatives. Benchmarking twelve pipeline configurations exposes a screening bottleneck: despite 90.9% retrieval recall at K=200, no system recovered more than 52.7% of ground-truth included literature.

title: Wear OS 7 arrives to Pixel watches with Gemini AI and live updates
blurb: Google released Wear OS 7 for the Pixel Watch 2, 3, and 4, adding Gemini-powered features including a Create My Widget tool that builds custom dashboards from natural language and multi-step automation for tasks like restaurant orders. The update also brings Live Updates for game scores and delivery tracking, connected control for earbuds and Android XR glasses, and a roughly 10% battery life improvement.

title: SkillVetBench: LLM-as-Judge for Multi-Dimensional Security Risk Evaluation in Open-Source LLM Agent Skills
blurb: SkillVetBench is a public Hugging Face leaderboard that uses an LLM-as-Judge to vet open-source agent skills for instruction-layer threats like prompt injection and memory poisoning that code-level scanners miss. It scores skills on SARS, a five-dimensional agentic-risk metric, and reports zero false negatives across 78 malicious skills versus the 15% the best static baseline misses.

title: Google Open-Sources a Knowledge Format and Wires It Into Its Catalog
blurb: Google Cloud published the Open Knowledge Format on June 12, a 451-line specification that represents AI-agent knowledge as a directory of plain markdown files requiring a single frontmatter field. The same day, Google updated its Knowledge Catalog to ingest OKF and serve it to agents, leaving storage, serving, query, and access control as the paid layer the spec explicitly marks out of scope.

title: From Tokens to Policy: Causal and Interpretable Heterogeneous Treatment Effects Identification
blurb: This paper introduces NEXIS (Neural EXposure Interaction Search), an iterative method that reframes heterogeneous treatment effect identification as a Markov-blanket discovery problem over multi-modal pre-treatment representations. The authors deployed it on two anti-poverty programs in Africa, using satellite imagery to capture previously unmeasured environmental effect modifiers and producing interpretable, prescriptive guidelines for the programs' next iterations.

title: Radical Numerics launches with $50M to build general biological intelligence
blurb: Radical Numerics launched with $50M in funding to build what it calls general biological intelligence and to scale its models and hire frontier AI talent. The lab comes from the team behind Evo, the first AI model able to read genomic sequences, and the broader field of generative genomics.

title: FEnc$^2$: Unifying Data Packing for Efficient Private Inference via Convolution and Architecture-Aware Fragment Encoding
blurb: FEnc² is a fragment-based encoding framework for CKKS private convolutional neural network inference that optimizes ciphertext packing to cut homomorphic operations by one to two orders of magnitude. Its Conv-aware Encoding and Arch-aware ciphertext compression deliver end-to-end speedups over Orion of up to 228x on GPU for LeNet on MNIST and up to 4.55x on GPU for MobileNet on ImageNet.

title: Rio de Janeiro's city government model Rio3.5 beats Qwen3.7 in recent benchmarks
blurb: Rio de Janeiro's city government released Rio3.5, a model that outperforms Qwen3.7 on recent benchmarks.

title: Malaysia’s AI agent-powered messaging app Respond.io raises $62.5M, eyes acquisitions
blurb: Respond.io, a Kuala Lumpur-based customer conversation platform, raised $62.5M in a Series B led by Camber Partners to fund acquisitions in North America and Europe. The company reached $35M in annual recurring revenue at a 30% profit margin, growing 169% year-over-year while using AI agents to handle inquiries, qualify leads, and close sales across channels like WhatsApp and Instagram.

title: Medical Heuristic Learning: An LLM-Driven Framework for Interpretable and Auditable Clinical Decision Rules
blurb: This paper introduces Medical Heuristic Learning (MHL), an LLM-driven framework that replaces neural weight updates with versioned pure-Python decision rules synthesized from statistical probes, medical knowledge, and iterative code refinement. The authors report performance comparable to state-of-the-art methods on clinical tabular data while staying fully auditable and resisting catastrophic forgetting in small-sample, imbalanced, and feature-drifting settings.

title: The Importance of Phase in Neural Representations: An Internal Oppenheim-Lim Test of Image Classifiers
blurb: This paper tests whether trained image classifiers encode object identity in Fourier phase rather than magnitude, transplanting one image's phase onto another's magnitude at a chosen layer and recording which image the prediction follows. Across PRISM2D, GFNet, and ViT-B/16 the readout tracks the phase or sign donor while image-specific magnitude is largely dispensable, giving a mechanistic account of the texture-shape gap between CNNs and attention models.

title: State Attorneys General Are Investigating OpenAI
blurb: A coalition of state attorneys general has opened an investigation into OpenAI, examining its handling of user data, protections for minors, and advertising practices. OpenAI confirmed the probe, which spans a wide range of its operations and adds to mounting regulatory scrutiny of the company's business and safety commitments.

title: SpaceX purchases Cursor, a Claude Code and OpenAI Codex competitor [U]
blurb: SpaceX acquired Cursor, the AI coding tool, in a roughly $60B deal that follows an earlier $10B compute partnership struck in April. The acquisition gives Cursor access to xAI's Colossus infrastructure to scale model training and strengthens its position against Anthropic's Claude Code and OpenAI's Codex.

title: MAGE-RAG: Multigranular Adaptive Graph Evidence for Agentic Multimodal RAG in Long-Document QA
blurb: MAGE-RAG is a multimodal RAG framework for long-document QA that builds an offline evidence graph of page and element nodes, then uses an online controller to activate, open, search, and prune evidence under explicit budgets at query time. It scores 52.75 accuracy on LongDocURL and 53.26 accuracy with 51.19 F1 on MMLongBench-Doc, balancing dispersed evidence coverage against context noise better than fixed Top-k text or page-level visual retrieval.

title: Pentagon boasts of using AI to write reports mandated by Congress
blurb: Pentagon CTO Emil Michael told the Hudson Institute that the Department of Defense is using generative AI to draft the hundreds of reports Congress mandates each year, cutting one report from 200 staff hours to five. The DoD has offered AI tools to all six military branches through its GenAI.mil platform, starting with Google Cloud's Gemini for Government, since December 2025.

title: Siri AI Catching Up to Chatbot Competitors
blurb: Apple's revamped Siri AI has closed much of the gap with leading chatbot competitors, according to Bloomberg's Mark Gurman. While the assistant largely delivers on features promised two years ago, Gurman says it is good enough to help pull Apple out of its prolonged AI crisis.

title: Judge Dismisses Elon Musk’s xAI Trade-Secret Lawsuit Against OpenAI
blurb: A federal judge dismissed Elon Musk's xAI lawsuit accusing OpenAI of stealing trade secrets by poaching an engineer who worked on its chatbot. The ruling removes one front in the broader legal fight between Musk and OpenAI, though xAI may still amend and refile the complaint.

title: A Multi-Center Benchmark for Abdominal Disease Diagnosis and Report Generation from Non-Contrast CT
blurb: This paper introduces a multi-center benchmark for diagnosing abdominal disease and generating radiology reports from single-phase non-contrast CT, avoiding the kidney risks and added workload of contrast-enhanced scans. Trained on paired NCCT-CECT studies from two centers and tested across five deep learning architectures, the approach retained diagnostic signal at an average multi-organ AUC of 69.1% internally and 63.1% on external validation, with the dataset and code released publicly.

title: Trump Administration Reignites Its Feud With Anthropic Over Latest A.I. Models
blurb: The Trump administration imposed surprise restrictions Friday that cut off foreign access to Anthropic's latest AI models, reigniting its conflict with the company. The move triggered another round of finger pointing between the administration and Anthropic over the rules governing model exports.

title: Cybersecurity experts don’t think Anthropic’s Fable 5 presents a unique threat
blurb: Cybersecurity practitioners said Anthropic's Fable 5 does not provide unique hacking capabilities, with dozens calling proposed export controls on the model's foreign use misguided. Recent jailbreak reports failed to demonstrate offensive capabilities beyond what existing models and tools already offer, undercutting the case that Fable 5 warrants distinct regulatory treatment.

title: Probably raises $9M to build a more reliable kind of AI
blurb: Probably raised $9M in seed funding led by Andreessen Horowitz to catch hallucinations and factual errors before they reach users, targeting the 99.99% accuracy typical of deterministic systems. Its data science tool pairs an LLM with a deterministic validator that rejects results not matching the dataset, letting it run on models four classes weaker than the frontier on local hardware.

title: Latent space mapping of interpretable structural coordinates from stochastic single-molecule signals
blurb: This paper introduces a contrastive encoder, trained only on simulated signals from a physics-informed model, that maps solid-state nanopore signals from engineered DNA barcodes into an interpretable molecular coordinate system. The representation stays invariant to acquisition conditions and translocation conformation, enabling data pooling across devices and single-pass molecule identification that cuts computational cost by three orders of magnitude versus alignment-based methods.

title: Anthropic reserves right to check ID for Claude subs
blurb: Anthropic updated its Claude terms to reserve the right to request government-issued ID for age or identity verification on its subscription plans. The clause gives the company latitude to enforce age requirements and regional compliance rules, though Anthropic has not said when or how often it would actually demand identification from users.

title: A year after Meta tapped Alexandr Wang to build a new AI model, Zuckerberg has to sell it
blurb: Meta spent over $14B last year to hire Alexandr Wang and his top Scale AI engineers, who delivered Muse Spark in April, the company's first proprietary foundation model. Now Zuckerberg has to prove Meta can attract paying users beyond its ad business, as the stock sits down 18% over the past 12 months despite 33% first-quarter revenue growth.

title: OpenAI hit with sweeping probe from massive coalition of 42 US state attorneys general just days after reported IPO filing — subpoena targets ChatGPT maker’s ads, data practices, handling of minors, model sycophancy, and safety policies
blurb: A coalition of 42 US state attorneys general served OpenAI with a sweeping subpoena on June 12, led by New York AG Letitia James, seeking documents on its advertising, data practices, handling of minors, and model sycophancy. The probe landed five days after OpenAI confidentially filed for an IPO reportedly valuing the company at up to $1 trillion, and adds to a civil suit Florida filed against OpenAI and Sam Altman on June 1.

title: VAST Data and the evolving neoclouds
blurb: VAST Data has become the dominant storage supplier to neoclouds, landing a 600 PB capacity win at Australia's Sharon AI. The GPU-focused cloud providers collectively booked $23B to $25B in 2025 revenue, more than triple their 2024 total, and are now expanding into sovereign datacenters and managed services as enterprise AI inferencing broadens their customer base beyond a few large model-training clients.

title: MA-SBI: Misspecification-Aware Simulation-Based Inference via Side-Channel Guidance
blurb: MA-SBI is a calibration-free framework for simulation-based inference that corrects for simulator misspecification using unstructured side-information like regime labels and policy bulletins rather than ground-truth parameter pairs. A learned corrector maps side-channel text to an observation-space shift applied before any pre-trained amortized posterior, requiring no retraining, and it matches the oracle posterior on hide-the-calibration benchmarks while improving log-likelihood on real COVID epidemiological data.

title: The Complexity of Min-Max Optimization for Quadratic Polynomials
blurb: This paper proves that computing approximate stationary points of min-max optimization over the hypercube is PPAD-hard for quadratic polynomials. The result holds even when the polynomials are multilinear, each variable appears in at most three monomials, and the approximation factor is inverse polynomial, yielding the first PPAD-hardness results for two-team zero-sum polymatrix games.

title: Upper Bounds on the Generalization Error of Deep Learning Models via Local Robustness and Stability
blurb: This paper introduces a generalization bound for deep learning models that scales the robustness term by the count of stable and unstable samples within each sub-region of the input space, rather than treating robustness as a single global measure. On ImageNet-trained models, the bounds stay non-vacuous and produce the tightest error estimates among existing methods, closely tracking empirical performance across a range of robust networks.

title: Dynestyx: A Probabilistic Programming Library for Dynamical Systems
blurb: Dynestyx is a probabilistic programming library with first-class support for state-space models, the standard formalism for Bayesian treatment of dynamical systems. Through a single unified interface, users can specify arbitrary priors for discrete-time or continuous-time systems, run inference over mixed-effect data, and produce state and parameter estimates with principled uncertainty quantification.

title: Scalable Pairwise Kernel Learning with Stochastic Vec Trick
blurb: This paper introduces SPaiK, a scalable kernel learning method for pairwise prediction that preserves the expressive power of kernel methods while cutting computational and memory costs. The core technique is the stochastic generalized vec trick, a stochastic extension of sparse Kronecker product multiplication that enables large-scale training, evaluated against state-of-the-art methods on seven real-world drug-target affinity datasets.

title: Does Traversal Order Matter? A Systematic Study of Tree Traversal Methods in Transformer Grammars
blurb: Transformer Grammars improve language modeling by linearizing syntactic trees, but prior work has only used Depth-First Traversal. This paper systematically evaluates Breadth-First Traversal and a novel hybrid, Production-Rule Traversal, across language modeling, syntactic generalization, and summarization, revealing trade-offs between nested composition and global lookahead and offering task-aware design recommendations.

title: Rethinking Scaffolding in LLM Tutors: The Interactional Mismatch Between Benchmarks and Real-World Deployments
blurb: This paper introduces an evaluation pipeline with two metrics, Chatbot Scaffolding and Student Uptake, applied across nine datasets of 9,490 chats spanning AI tutor benchmarks and real-world educational chatbot deployments. The authors find that while benchmarks assume students take up the chatbot's graduated guidance, real-world students frequently bypass that pedagogical framing to pursue their own learning goals, arguing future benchmarks should evaluate how tutors handle student-driven interaction rather than assuming uptake.

title: Arcade.dev Raises $60 Million to Secure AI Agents
blurb: Arcade.dev raised $60M to help companies control which actions their AI agents are authorized to take. The platform addresses agent authorization and access management, a growing security gap as enterprises deploy autonomous agents that can execute tasks across internal systems and external services.

title: MuVAP: Multimodal Multiparty Voice Activity Projection for Turn-taking Prediction in the Wild
blurb: MuVAP is a causal multimodal framework that extends Voice Activity Projection to predict turn-taking in multiparty conversations using only a monaural audio stream and a single camera view, grounding acoustic predictions in face tracks. Its Role-Relative Projection maps any N-speaker interaction onto a fixed current-versus-next floor-holder state, and it outperforms strong baselines on Shift-Hold and next-speaker prediction across two- and three-speaker settings.

title: CrossMaps: Confidence-Aware Open-Vocabulary Semantic Mapping for Rover Navigation
blurb: CrossMaps is a real-time, confidence-aware open-vocabulary semantic mapping pipeline that builds language-queryable maps from RGB-D data to guide rover navigation. It pairs multi-scale CLIP embeddings with a dual-memory architecture, where short-term memory aggregates noisy observations using geometric, semantic, and temporal confidence cues and promotes coherent cells to long-term memory as persistent landmarks, running on a Jetson Orin UGV alongside SLAM.

title: Contaminated Collaboration: Measuring Gender Bias Transfer in LLM-Assisted Student Writing
blurb: This paper tests whether gender bias in an LLM writing assistant transfers into student writing, finding that students using a biased assistant produced career plan essays with a significantly larger agentic gap and more gender-stereotypic occupation suggestions than control and neutral groups. The transfer was asymmetric across 123 participants, suppressing agency in essays about female profiles while leaving male-target writing largely unaffected.

title: AI agent authorization startup Arcade nabs $60M investment
blurb: Arcade AI raised $60M in a Series A led by SYN Ventures, with Morgan Stanley and Wipro joining, to expand its authorization platform for AI agents. The platform integrates with companies' identity providers to grant agents scoped access to specific application features rather than blanket permissions, narrowing the attack surface if an agent is compromised.

title: GPU infrastructure management startup Hydra Host raises $100M
blurb: Hydra Host raised $100M in a Series A led by Kindred Ventures, with participation from Nvidia, Founders Fund, Ark Invest, and others, to expand its GPU-as-a-service platform. The company runs a marketplace that pools GPUs from data center operators worldwide, and its Brokkr AI Factory Operating System has been deployed across more than 50 data centers.

title: The embrace of open science: An analysis of a decade of AI research and 56 800 conference papers
blurb: This paper analyzes 56,800 papers from five leading AI conferences to track how documentation practices changed between 2014 and 2024. The share of papers sharing both code and data rose nearly sixfold, from 11% to 64%, and the authors estimate reproducibility climbed from 28% to 64% over the decade. Notably, those gains predate the reproducibility checklists major venues introduced, suggesting cultural shifts in the field drove the improvement rather than top-down policy.

title: Ent Security, which wants to build a new layer of workspace security that reads the intent behind what users and AI agents do, launches with $100M in funding (Duncan Riley/SiliconANGLE)
blurb: Ent Security launched with $100M in funding to build a workspace security layer that interprets the intent behind actions taken by users and AI agents. The approach aims to move beyond rule-based controls by flagging risky behavior based on what an actor is trying to accomplish, rather than matching against predefined signatures.

title: A case for how to shape ‘ingredient lists’ for AI models
blurb: A policy paper from the Institute for Security and Technology makes the case for AI bills of materials (AIBOMs), inventories of the models, datasets, and components used to build and operate AI systems, as a way to reduce cyber risk and improve supply chain transparency. The authors argue standards must be developed from both supply and demand sides before wide adoption, warning that uncoordinated efforts could fragment tooling and weaken policy.

title: Anthropic Shutdown Shows Washington’s New Test for AI Launches
blurb: Anthropic disabled Fable 5 and Mythos 5 globally on June 12 after a Commerce Department order required suspending access for foreign nationals, including some of its own employees, which the company said it could not enforce selectively. Administration officials framed the deemed-export dispute as a test of national-security judgment, signaling that future AI launches face a political review beyond red-team results.

title: Exploding and vanishing gradients in deep neural networks: the effect of residual connections
blurb: This paper analyzes exploding and vanishing gradients in deep neural networks using multiplicative ergodic theory, explaining how residual connections alter the dynamics. The authors apply Furstenberg and Kifer's characterization of Liapunov exponents to make a precise statement about the Liapunov spectrum and how adding a residual connection shifts it.

title: Process-Oriented Evaluation of AI-Assisted Scientific Writing
blurb: This paper analyzes 869 keystroke-level edit logs and finds AI-generated abstracts gain sentence-level agency but lag human-authored ones on global coherence.

title: Green SARC: Predictive Cost and Carbon Governance for Agentic AI Systems
blurb: Green SARC applies the SARC governance-by-architecture framework to FinOps and GreenOps, enforcing cost and carbon budgets at four sites inside the agent loop rather than on dashboards reviewed after execution. On 3,000 real multi-step plans, the architectural gate kept over-budget incidence at 0% where a soft Lagrangian penalty breached budgets on 91.5% of seeds, and end-to-end token, dollar, and carbon savings reached 47–55%.

title: Graphical-Probabilistic Modeling of Generative Flows in LLM-Native Software Systems
blurb: This paper proposes Generation Networks, a graphical probabilistic modeling framework for documenting generative flows and stating design-level properties of LLM-native software systems. The approach aims to bring modularity and abstraction to a field that currently relies on low-level prompting and context engineering, capturing the stochastic, prompt-dependent behavior and emergent phenomena that traditional software engineering methods cannot express.

title: Measuring Whether LLM Tutors Teach or Solve: A Diagnostic for Educational Impact
blurb: This paper proposes a diagnostic for whether LLM tutors actually support learning rather than just producing answers, based on the gap between solving-oriented and pedagogy-oriented benchmark scores. Across eight models on the MathTutorBench leaderboard, the authors found a 0.421 correlation between solving and pedagogy composites, with several models shifting rank when evaluation moves from one to the other.

title: Qualcomm mulls taking over Jim Keller's Tenstorrent, report claims — deal for AI chipmaker would value the company at between $8 billion and $10 billion
blurb: Qualcomm is in talks to acquire Jim Keller's Tenstorrent at a valuation between $8B and $10B, more than double the $3.2B the AI chipmaker sought from investors last year. The pull is Tenstorrent's RISC-V AI accelerators and data center CPU IP, but the fit is messy: Qualcomm would end up with two AI accelerator lines and three data center CPU designs after already buying Ventana and Alphawave. The pattern is familiar for Qualcomm, which used Nuvia and Atheros to buy its way into entire new markets, and it is now paying frontier prices to stay in the AI silicon race.

title: “단일 AI 모델=새로운 단일 장애 지점”…앤트로픽 접근 차단이 던진 경고
blurb: The US government ordered Anthropic to disable Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all foreign nationals, citing export-control and national-security concerns the company calls thinly supported. Any organization that wired core processes, coding tools, or cyber defense directly to a single US AI model just discovered a new single point of failure, one that depends on which government can cut access and under what conditions. Model accuracy and security are no longer enough to evaluate. Switching costs make most customers stay put anyway, but the episode hands European and sovereign-AI advocates their clearest argument yet.

title: ChatGPT’s market share slips below 50% for first time
blurb: ChatGPT's share of the AI assistant market fell below 50% for the first time, even as it stays the most-used product with over 1.1 billion monthly users. Gemini now sits at 662 million and Claude at 245 million, so the gap is closing from both the consumer and enterprise sides. The takeaway for founders building on top of these models is that the assistant layer is becoming a real multi-horse race, which changes distribution math, default integrations, and who you bet your roadmap on.

title: Endpoint Security Startup Ent Emerges From Stealth With $100 Million Seed Round
blurb: Ent emerged from stealth with a $100M seed round led by Decibel to put real-time AI reasoning on the endpoint, judging what a user or agent intends before a risky action goes through. Traditional EDR logs and responds after the damage is done, which breaks as autonomous agents run workflows that look exactly like legitimate work. The bet from RiskIQ founders Elias Manousos and Brandon Dixon is that intent, evaluated at the moment of operation across both people and AI agents, becomes the security layer enterprises buy next.

title: Databricks agrees to acquire Panther Labs, its third cybersecurity acquisition; Panther was valued at $1.4B when it raised a $120M Series B in 2021 (Jeffrey Dastin/Reuters)
blurb: Databricks agreed to acquire Panther Labs, its third cybersecurity deal as it builds out a security stack around its data platform. Panther, a cloud-native SIEM, was valued at $1.4B when it raised a $120M Series B in 2021, so the price likely reflects a reset from peak-cycle valuations. The pattern is clear: the big data and AI platforms are absorbing security tooling directly, which squeezes the standalone vendors building on top of them and raises the bar for any startup selling point solutions in that layer.

title: SpaceX rises 12% to leapfrog Amazon, briefly top Microsoft in market cap
blurb: SpaceX rose about 12% to briefly touch a $2.94T market cap, passing Amazon and edging out Microsoft to become the fourth-largest US company days after its IPO. The stock is up roughly 62% from its $135 IPO price, more than Amazon gained in five years, even though SpaceX lost $4.9B on $18.7B of revenue in 2025. The rally rides on Musk's claim of $1T revenue by 2030 and a $60B deal for Cursor, but CFRA already rates it a sell with a price target 29% below Friday's close.

title: Snap unveils $2,195 AR glasses as CEO Evan Spiegel bets on post-smartphone future
blurb: Snap released Specs, a $2,195 pair of see-through AR glasses aimed at consumers, with CEO Evan Spiegel betting people are tired enough of smartphone screens to pay 15 times what its failed 2016 Spectacles cost. The problem is that Meta and Google are funding the same bet with profits from dominant ad businesses, while Snap has lost money every year as a public company and shed 4% on the news. The post-smartphone hardware race is now a balance-sheet contest, and Snap is the only contender without the cash machine to absorb a long, expensive miss.

title: Leaked financial docs show OpenAI is losing billions of dollars a year
blurb: Leaked audited financials reviewed ahead of OpenAI's expected IPO show revenue jumping from $3.7B in 2024 to $13.07B in 2025, with monthly revenue nearing $2B by year-end. But spending outran it, as operating losses widened from $8.78B to $20.92B, driven by $19.18B in R&D and a sales and marketing line that grew fivefold to $5.73B. The one bright spot is that losses shrank as a share of revenue, from 237% to 160%, which still leaves a long road to the profitability OpenAI is promising investors by 2030.

title: SpaceX stock jumps 20% in first full day of trading after record debut
blurb: SpaceX rose 20% in its first full day of public trading, pushing its valuation past $2 trillion after a record IPO debut. The jump makes SpaceX one of the most valuable companies in the world and gives Elon Musk a publicly traded counterweight to Tesla. For founders, it resets the ceiling on what a capital-intensive hardware company can be worth, after a decade of investors favoring asset-light software over launch vehicles and satellites.

title: DeepSeek Becomes China’s Most Valuable AI Startup After $7.4 Billion Fundraise
blurb: DeepSeek raised more than $7.4 billion in its first outside round, making it China's most valuable AI startup. The round funds the kind of compute-heavy training that has gotten far more expensive since the company's cheap-to-run models first rattled the market last year. China now has a clearly capitalized national champion to put against the US frontier labs, and the cost narrative that made DeepSeek famous matters less than the war chest it just built.

title: Fox Buys Roku, The Problem With Fox’s Smart Strategy, Streaming That Works
blurb: Fox acquired Roku, and the market sold off the deal, but the logic is that Fox is trading its old position extracting fees from rights holders for the leverage of controlling distribution. By owning the operating system that sits between viewers and every streaming app, Fox becomes the renter's landlord rather than another tenant fighting for a slot. The problem is that the strategy is smart on paper but depends on Fox executing as a platform company, a very different muscle from running a content business.

title: Fox’s $22B Roku acquisition aims to expand its reach into smart TVs, advertising
blurb: Fox Corporation agreed to buy Roku for $22B, or $160 per share, uniting Fox's broadcast and Tubi streaming with Roku's 100 million households, ad business, and operating system. The hardware loses money, but Roku's advertising arm pulled in $371M last quarter and its OS is the real asset. On a pro forma basis the combined company becomes the third-largest player in US TV by share of viewing, behind only YouTube and Disney, as the streaming wars consolidate around ad-supported distribution and the operating system that owns the screen.

title: US Offers Conditional $500 Million Loan for Rare-Earths Plant
blurb: The Pentagon's Office of Strategic Capital offered mining startup Phoenix Tailings a conditional $500M in long-term debt to build a domestic rare-earth processing plant. The money flows through a defense channel, not a venture one, signaling that Washington now treats supply-chain independence as a national-security line item it will underwrite directly. For founders in hard tech, the takeaway is that the cheapest capital for capital-intensive builds increasingly comes from agencies trying to reshore critical materials, not from VCs.

title: Mobileye’s US robotaxi launch will put it on both sides of the AV business
