blurb: SailPoint acquired AI agent security startup Entro for a reported $200M to extend its Agentic Fabric product, which brings access controls to non-human and AI agent identities. The deal adds Entro's credential discovery, behavioral monitoring, and automated remediation, giving SailPoint out-of-the-box coverage for over 1,000 agent types and discovery of more than 1,200 non-human identity types such as keys and tokens.

title: How Siemens "slices the elephant," advancing agentic workflows for industrial software development
blurb: Siemens and Google Cloud built Knowledge Fabric, an agentic system that ingests Siemens' codebases of hundreds of millions of lines into knowledge graphs on Spanner Graph, paired with Gemini, the Agent Development Kit, Gemini CLI, and Anthropic's Claude Code. In a pilot migrating legacy frontends to web-based interfaces, the system cut implementation effort while preserving full system compatibility, signaling Google Cloud's push to win large enterprise modernization workloads.

title: Project Jupiter AI data center build raises concerns about water usage in rural New Mexico desert — Oracle calls water usage 'negligible' for 11 million gallon one-time fill
blurb: Oracle's 1,400-acre Project Jupiter data center in New Mexico's Doña Ana County has drawn local opposition over water use, though Oracle calls its closed-loop cooling system "negligible" at an 11-million-gallon one-time fill plus 4,000 gallons annually for top-offs. The dispute lands in a desert region already ordered to cut groundwater use by 5.9 billion gallons per year under a Supreme Court Rio Grande ruling.

title: Microsoft brings Planner Agent to all Microsoft 365 Copilot users
blurb: Microsoft made Planner Agent generally available to all Microsoft 365 Copilot license holders, letting them create, update, and prioritize tasks through natural language across Teams, Loop, and SharePoint. AI-generated plans land in draft mode for human approval before execution, and Microsoft routes complex requests like building plans from Office files to a more capable model while handling simple tasks with a lighter one.

title: Cyber threats stalking the 2026 World Cup
blurb: A Darktrace report released ahead of the 2026 World Cup found that 57% of professional sports organizations experienced multiple cyber incidents in the past 12 months, with 83% of their security staff saying they detected AI use in attacks against them. The average incident cost sports organizations roughly $169,000, a figure the firm expects to climb during the 48-team tournament as live-event timing widens the window for attackers.

title: KPMG pulls report on AI usage due to apparent hallucinations
blurb: KPMG pulled its October 2025 report "Redefining excellence in the age of agentic AI" after UBS, the UK's NHS, Swiss Federal Railways, and Transport for London said its claims about their AI deployments were untrue or misleading. Research group GPTZero traced the inaccuracies to AI hallucinations, marking the second Big Four AI-generated report withdrawn in a month after EY pulled one with fabricated footnotes.

title: Windows 11 users are tired of MS account requirements creeping into everything
blurb: Microsoft continues requiring a Microsoft account during Windows 11 setup, with no in-box option to create a local account, a complaint that resurfaced in a widely shared Reddit thread despite the company's feedback-driven Windows K2 initiative. The requirement ties device setup to cloud services and BitLocker recovery-key escrow, but IT buyers and users criticize the lack of transparency and control, pushing many toward Rufus and command-line workarounds.

title: Arcade.dev Raises $60 Million to Secure AI Agents
blurb: Arcade.dev raised $60M to help enterprises control which actions their AI agents are authorized to take, addressing the authorization gap that has slowed agent deployment in production. The funding pushes Arcade into a crowded agent-security market alongside identity incumbents like Okta and Microsoft Entra, betting that fine-grained, per-action permissions become a standalone procurement line rather than a feature bundled into existing IAM stacks.

title: AMD buys data center memory optimization startup Mext
blurb: AMD acquired memory-optimization startup Mext for an undisclosed sum to address DRAM supply constraints hitting AI data centers. Mext's AI-native tiering engine moves cold data to cheaper NAND flash and predictively pulls it back into DRAM before it's needed, increasing usable memory and lowering total cost of ownership for cloud providers and enterprises running on existing hardware.

title: NHS Palantir claims face scrutiny after data suggests uneven results
blurb: Reporting on NHS England's claims for Palantir's Federated Data Platform faces scrutiny after performance data suggests the rollout has delivered uneven results across trusts, with gains concentrated in a handful of sites rather than system-wide. The findings raise questions about the £330M contract's return as NHS leadership weighs further expansion, and they hand ammunition to critics already challenging Palantir's role in handling patient data.

title: Tech debt, process gaps keep firms in AI ‘pilot purgatory,’ study finds
blurb: Tech debt and broken processes are stalling enterprise AI deployments in "pilot purgatory," with the world's top 2,000 public firms sitting on nearly $18 trillion in untapped AI value, according to Genpact research. The findings point at internal weaknesses rather than the technology itself, suggesting IT leaders need to fix process and infrastructure gaps before AI investments convert to returns.

title: Microsoft's Copilot Cowork now generally available with usage-based billing
blurb: Microsoft made Copilot Cowork generally available worldwide, adding usage-based billing through Copilot Credits priced at $0.01 each and metered on model use, context retrieval, tool calls, and runtime. The agentic feature runs long-running tasks on Anthropic's Opus 4.8 and Sonnet 4.6, requires a Microsoft 365 Copilot subscription, and ships with spending limits, alerts, and prepaid plans to give IT teams cost control.

title: AI 에이전트는 늘어나는데 통제는 없다…CIO 덮친 거버넌스 공백
blurb: An IBM Institute for Business Value survey found two-thirds of CIOs and CTOs are accountable for AI systems they don't fully control, as employees and business units build and deploy their own agents outside IT oversight. Respondents expect agent counts to grow 38% over the next year, yet only one in 10 say they are prepared for the sprawl.

title: Caddy compatibility for zeroserve: 3x throughput and 70% lower latency
blurb: zeroserve shipped a Caddy compatibility layer that reads existing Caddyfile configs, letting teams swap in the reverse proxy without rewriting their setup. The project reports 3x higher throughput and 70% lower P99 latency than Caddy in its benchmarks, with the migration path aimed at drop-in replacement for existing deployments.

title: AI demands more engineering discipline. Not less (xpost)
blurb: Charity Majors argues that AI-assisted development raises the bar for engineering discipline rather than lowering it, drawing a parallel to the shift from hand-maintained server pets to immutable infrastructure. The piece contends that generating more code faster makes review, testing, observability, and ownership boundaries more important, since teams now ship volume they did not personally write and must still operate in production.

title: Improving Arm64 support in CNCF projects with OCI credits
blurb: The CNCF detailed how it's using Oracle Cloud Infrastructure credits to expand Arm64 build and test coverage across its projects, addressing a gap as Arm instances now make up over 50% of new AWS launches and a third on Azure. The credits give maintainers native Arm64 CI runners, removing the emulation overhead and cross-compilation toil that previously slowed multi-arch release pipelines.

title: Linux 7.1
blurb: Linus Torvalds released Linux 7.1, the first point release on the 7.x line, with the announcement landing on the LKML as the usual end-of-cycle summary of merged changes. Platform and SRE teams should track the included scheduler, networking, and driver updates against their distro's backport timeline, since kernel-level fixes here shape host stability and performance across production fleets.

title: Beyond Usability: A UX Case Study on Using "Withdrawal Design" to Challenge Engagement Metrics in Social Robotics
blurb: A UX case study on social robots for autistic children argues that high engagement and usability scores can mask ecological harm. In an 8-week home RCT (N=40), keeping a consumer robot lowered anxiety but was tied to weaker gains in social motivation and emotion recognition, while withdrawal sometimes pushed children toward human interaction. The takeaway for PMs: "engagement" is an output metric that can siphon behavior into the product itself, so success should be judged by outcomes beyond the product, not stickiness alone.

title: The hidden pattern behind successful products | Mark Pincus (founder of Zynga)
blurb: In a Lenny's Podcast interview, Mark Pincus argues that founders' instincts are usually right while their specific product ideas are usually wrong, and offers a "Proven, Better, New" framework for separating the two. The idea is to anchor a product in something already proven in the market, make it meaningfully better on one dimension, and add one genuinely new element, rather than betting on novelty across the board.

title: did:crdt: Coordination-Free Decentralised Identifiers via Signed CRDTs
blurb: This paper introduces did:crdt, a Decentralised Identifier method that updates DID documents without any ledger, sequencer, or global ordering, composing each document from signed Conflict-Free Replicated Data Types that merge deterministically on concurrent edits. The design eliminates the fees, latency, and consensus requirements of blockchain-anchored and Sidetree methods, needing only causal delivery of signed deltas to keep replicas convergent.

title: Wall Street Stares Down Crypto-Style Trading All Day, Every Day
blurb: Traditional finance firms are moving toward 24/7 trading across a widening range of assets, adopting the round-the-clock schedule that has defined crypto markets for years. The shift erodes a structural divide between TradFi and crypto, pressuring exchanges, market makers, and clearing infrastructure built around fixed trading hours to support continuous operation.

title: Kraken and Coinbase Bring Perps Onshore as US Derivatives Markets Shift
blurb: Kraken and Coinbase each launched perpetual futures products on Monday, with Kraken going live on CFTC-regulated crypto perps through Bitnomial and Coinbase rolling out four thematic equity-index perpetuals on US soil. The moves bring a market structure that has lived offshore onto regulated American venues, opening domestic access to leveraged derivatives that previously drove traders to platforms like Binance and Bybit.

title: Sui Processes $65 Billion in Stablecoin Transfers in Five Days After Zeroing Out Fees
blurb: Sui processed nearly $65B in stablecoin transfers in the five days since June 10, when it removed gas fees on stablecoin transactions to position the Layer 1 as a fee-free settlement rail. The gasless design targets institutional and agentic payment flows, where per-transaction costs otherwise compound across high-volume automated transfers.

title: India files charges against 8 defendants in alleged $20 million Coinbase spoofing scam
blurb: India's Directorate of Enforcement filed charges against Chirag Tomar and seven other individuals and entities over an alleged Coinbase spoofing scheme that used cloned login pages to steal more than $20 million in crypto. The agency has provisionally attached 64.55 crore rupees ($6.8 million) in assets, while Tomar already faces a 60-month US prison sentence tied to the same fraud.

title: Coinbase CEO Armstrong Calls Accredited-Investor Rules a 'Regressive Tax'
blurb: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong called on the US government to overhaul its accredited-investor framework, arguing that the wealth-based thresholds, unchanged since the early 1980s, lock retail buyers out of private deals where most pre-IPO upside accrues. He proposed replacing the $1M net-worth and $200K income tests with either a competency exam or pure disclosure rules, echoing the House-passed INVEST Act now pending in the Senate.

title: Thetanuts Finance: $2.1M Attack, Partial White-Hat Recovery
blurb: Thetanuts Finance, the on-chain options and structured product protocol, was exploited for $2.1 million, with security firm Blockaid publishing the exploit transaction and attacker address shortly after the attack. The team recovered part of the stolen funds through a white-hat negotiation, limiting net losses below the initial drained amount.

title: MANTRA to be acquired by Inveniam, backer that invested $20 million last year
blurb: MANTRA agreed to be acquired by Inveniam, the asset-tokenization firm that invested $20 million in the company last year. The deal follows the 2025 collapse of MANTRA's OM token and a subsequent restructuring that included layoffs, handing control to a backer already exposed to the project.

title: Robust and Automated Reconfiguration of Byzantine Wide-Area Replication
blurb: This paper identifies three vulnerabilities in latency-based reconfiguration of BFT consensus, where replicas dynamically assign leaders and tune voting weights based on network monitoring. The authors propose Beware, a framework that filters falsified latency reports, computes robust weight distributions, and uses machine learning to converge on Byzantine-resilient configurations. The work matters for blockchain validator sets that optimize throughput through adaptive configuration, since unguarded tuning hands adversarial nodes a new attack surface.

title: Humanity Protocol Launches New H Token Airdrop After $36M Exploit
blurb: Humanity Protocol began a 1:1 airdrop of a newly audited H token to holders snapshotted on Ethereum, BNB Chain, and Humanity Mainnet just before the June 8 exploit that drained $36M. The token traded around $0.22 on Monday, down 73% from its June 2 peak, with addresses linked to the DPRK-affiliated attacker excluded from the distribution.

title: Bitcoin Tops $67,000 to Two-Week High After Trump Declares US-Iran Deal Complete and Hormuz Reopening
blurb: Bitcoin climbed past $67,000 to its highest level in roughly two weeks after President Trump declared the US-Iran deal "complete" and authorized reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Oil fell about 4% on the news, and the move liquidated around $150 million in crypto short positions.

title: Aztec Connect Drained of $2.1M Through Deprecated Contract Three Years After Shutdown
blurb: An attacker drained roughly $2.1M from a deprecated Aztec Connect contract on Sunday, three years after the privacy bridge was shut down, by exploiting a mismatch between its zero-knowledge proof verification and Ethereum settlement paths to credit balances never backed by real deposits. Aztec Labs holds no admin keys over the system and cannot pause, upgrade, or reverse the contracts, leaving the stolen ETH, DAI, and wstETH unrecoverable.

title: Ledn says bitcoin-backed lending market could reach $1 trillion as securitization attracts institutional capital
blurb: Ledn co-founder Mauricio Di Bartolomeo projected the bitcoin-backed lending market could reach $1 trillion within five to 10 years, arguing that securitizing loans into rated bonds would draw institutional capital the way mortgage and auto-loan markets already do. Ledn, which originated $1.4 billion in loans in 2025 for an estimated 30% market share, recently issued the first BTC debt instrument to earn an investment-grade rating from S&P Global.

title: Bitcoin DeFi demand concentrated in small-but-deep pockets, Rootstock exec says
blurb: Bitcoin DeFi demand is concentrated in a handful of small but deep liquidity pockets rather than spread broadly across protocols, according to a Rootstock executive. The comment lands as overall DeFi total value locked has fallen from roughly $180 billion to $70 billion in under a year, underscoring how thin the market has become outside a few active venues.

title: Bitcoin rises after Bank of Japan hikes interest rates to a 31-year high
blurb: The Bank of Japan raised its policy rate by 25 basis points to 1%, the highest level since 1995, while signaling further tightening if inflation accelerates. Bitcoin climbed from about $65,600 to $66,000 after the decision, as traders focused on the BOJ's move to pause its bond taper and fix monthly JGB purchases near 2 trillion yen rather than on the rate hike itself.

title: Bitmine adds another $136 million of ether after raising $274 million in preferred stock sale
blurb: BitMine bought 76,881 ETH worth roughly $136 million last week, lifting its treasury to 5.62 million ETH after closing a $274 million preferred stock offering that carries a 9.5% annualized dividend. The shares list on the NYSE under BMNP, applying Strategy's preferred-equity playbook to an Ethereum treasury, with Tom Lee citing staking revenue as support for the dividend payments.

title: Hyperliquid loses Anthropic, OpenAI markets as creator shuts down project
blurb: Ventuals, the team behind OpenAI and Anthropic perpetual futures on Hyperliquid, is winding down and folding into another project in the ecosystem, halting and auto-settling all positions in the OPENAI and ANTHROPIC markets. The shutdown, which followed more than $650M in lifetime volume, signals early consolidation among HIP-3 operators building third-party perp markets on the venue.

title: Forward Solana treasury stock jumps 8% as smaller DATs reject acquisition bids
blurb: Forward Inc. (FWDI) shares rose as much as 8.6% on Tuesday even as its unsolicited bids to acquire smaller Solana treasury firms HSDT, SKYA, and SLMT were rejected, with Solana Company's board calling the all-stock offer a substantial undervaluation. Forward, the largest SOL treasury holder at over 7 million tokens, offered 10%-30% premiums via 1:1 FWDI share swaps and faces a Russell 2000 and 3000 index inclusion at month-end.

title: Mystery Polymarket trader turned $4 million into $9 million after Spain's shocking World Cup draw
blurb: A days-old Polymarket wallet called "fishalive" turned roughly $4 million into about $9 million in profit after World Cup debutant Cabo Verde held favorite Spain to a 0-0 draw. The outsized win on an account created this month has drawn onchain scrutiny over whether it reflected luck or inside information, while another trader lost nearly $1 million backing Spain for an $85,000 potential payout.

title: French BTC treasury firm Capital B developing STRC-style bitcoin credit instrument
blurb: Capital B, the Paris-listed bitcoin treasury company trading on Euronext Growth under ALCPB, is developing a bitcoin-backed credit instrument for the European market modeled on Strategy's STRC and Strive's SATA, according to board director Alexandre Laizet. The firm is targeting double-digit yields with sub-10% volatility and says investor interest in the digital credit space has grown tenfold over the past year.

title: Wall Street Stares Down Crypto-Style Trading All Day, Every Day
blurb: Traditional finance firms are moving toward 24/7 trading across a widening range of assets, adopting the always-on schedule that has defined crypto markets for years. The shift raises operational questions for exchanges, clearinghouses, and settlement systems built around fixed market hours and overnight processing windows.

title: Sui Processes $65 Billion in Stablecoin Transfers in Five Days After Zeroing Out Fees
blurb: Sui processed nearly $65B in stablecoin transfers since June 10 after zeroing out gas fees for stablecoin movement, the Layer 1's gasless design absorbing transaction costs at the protocol level. The shift recasts Sui as a fee-free settlement rail aimed at institutional and agentic payments, where per-transaction cost has been a barrier to high-volume, machine-initiated flows.

title: Invisible Manipulation Channels in AI-Assisted Financial Advisory: Implications for Market Integrity and Regulatory Design
blurb: This paper identifies a manipulation channel at the sampling layer of LLM inference that lets adversaries systematically bias AI-generated credit and investment opinions while passing output-based audits, including statistical watermarking. The authors show detection is statistically hard, since the KL divergence between manipulated and normal output distributions can be driven arbitrarily small, forcing impractically large sample sizes. For regulators relying on output audits to police AI advisory, the implication is that integrity controls must move into the inference pipeline itself.

title: Kraken and Coinbase Bring Perps Onshore as US Derivatives Markets Shift
blurb: Kraken and Coinbase each launched perpetual futures products Monday, with Kraken going live on CFTC-regulated crypto contracts through Bitnomial and Coinbase introducing four thematic equity-index perpetuals on US soil. The moves bring a product long confined to offshore venues under domestic regulatory oversight, intensifying competition for US derivatives volume as the CFTC signals openness to onshore crypto perps.

title: Moody's: Private Credit Faces 2028 Test
blurb: Moody's Global Head of Private Credit Marc Pinto warned that the asset class faces a defining stress test in 2028, when a wave of refinancing will separate viable borrowers from distressed ones. AI infrastructure lending is fueling growth, but cracks are surfacing in software-focused portfolios, signaling that underwriting discipline matters more than headline deal volume.

title: SEC Chair Atkins defends CFTC’s Selig despite questions over agency’s ability to regulate prediction markets
blurb: SEC Chair Paul Atkins publicly backed CFTC nominee Michael Selig amid questions over whether the derivatives regulator has the statutory authority to oversee prediction markets. The defense signals the two agencies are aligning on a CFTC-led approach, which would put platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket under commodities oversight rather than securities rules and shape how event contracts are regulated.

title: Carvana is expanding into new vehicles. The implications could reshape the U.S. automotive retail market
blurb: Carvana has quietly acquired seven new-vehicle franchises selling Stellantis brands since last year, with its Casa Grande, Arizona store moving over 700 new cars last month to become Stellantis' top-volume location nationally. The expansion threatens the century-old franchised dealer system, a network of 16,990 retailers that booked over $1.3 trillion in sales last year, by bringing Carvana's online retail model to new-car sales.

title: Coinbase CEO Armstrong Calls Accredited-Investor Rules a 'Regressive Tax'
blurb: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong called on regulators to overhaul the SEC's accredited-investor framework, calling wealth-based limits on private investment a "regressive tax" that bars ordinary Americans from pre-IPO gains. He proposed replacing the $1M net-worth and $200K income thresholds with a competency exam or scrapping the gate entirely, echoing the House-passed INVEST Act now stalled in the Senate.

title: Ledn says bitcoin-backed lending market could reach $1 trillion as securitization attracts institutional capital
blurb: Ledn co-founder Mauricio Di Bartolomeo projected that the bitcoin-backed lending market could reach $1 trillion within five to 10 years if loans are securitized and sold to institutions, following Ledn's recent S&P-rated, investment-grade bond using Fidelity as custodian and Jefferies as bookrunner. Ledn estimates it holds 30% of the global consumer market, originating $1.4 billion in loans in 2025, though the sector remains shadowed by the 2022 collapses of Celsius, BlockFi, and Genesis.
